definition_en: A document given to a passenger to board a flight
example: You can download your boarding pass on your phone.

definition_en: The area in an airport where passengers wait before boarding
example: We waited in the departure lounge for two hours.

definition_en: Sudden, irregular movement of air that causes a plane to shake
example: Passengers were asked to fasten their seatbelts due to turbulence.

definition_en: A form stating what goods you are bringing into a country
example: You must fill in a customs declaration if you are carrying cash.

definition_en: The long strip of ground where aircraft take off and land
example: The plane waited on the runway before departure.

definition_en: Bags small enough to take into the cabin of a plane
example: Your hand luggage must fit in the overhead compartment.

definition_en: The screening process at airports to check for prohibited items
example: Allow extra time for the security check.

definition_en: A crew member who serves passengers on a flight
example: The flight attendant showed us the emergency exits.

definition_en: The area in an airport where passengers collect their checked bags
example: We waited 30 minutes at baggage claim.

definition_en: Passing through an airport or country on the way to another destination
example: She was in transit in Singapore for six hours.

definition_en: The numbered exit point in an airport from which passengers board a plane
example: Your flight departs from gate 14B.

definition_en: When a flight leaves later than scheduled
example: The flight suffered a two-hour delay due to bad weather.

definition_en: To sell more tickets than there are available seats
example: The airline overbooking meant some passengers were denied boarding.

definition_en: The official process of checking passports when entering a country
example: The queue at immigration took over an hour.

definition_en: A seat next to the aisle in a plane, bus or cinema
example: I always book an aisle seat so I can stretch my legs.

definition_en: The enclosed walkway connecting the terminal to the aircraft
example: Passengers boarded via the jet bridge directly from the terminal.

definition_en: A second flight taken to reach a final destination
example: My connecting flight was from Amsterdam to Buenos Aires.

definition_en: Luggage that exceeds the weight limit allowed by the airline
example: She had to pay for excess baggage at the check-in desk.

definition_en: A building at an airport where passengers depart and arrive
example: International flights depart from Terminal 3.

definition_en: The height of a plane above sea level
example: We reached a cruising altitude of 35,000 feet.

definition_en: The service that manages the movement of aircraft
example: Air traffic control cleared the plane for landing.

definition_en: The flight attendants who look after passengers on a flight
example: The cabin crew served drinks and snacks during the flight.
